title: Adina pilulifera
what_description: Adina pilulifera is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. It is a deciduous tree that grows up to 20 meters in height, with a straight trunk and a broad, rounded crown. The bark is smooth, grayish-brown, and peels off in small patches, while the leaves are opposite, simple, and elliptical, measuring 8-15 centimeters in length and 4-6 centimeters in width. The inflorescence is a terminal spike, bearing small, fragrant flowers that are white or yellowish in color, and the fruit is a capsule containing several seeds. The plant is native to tropical Africa, and its wood is valued for its durability and resistance to decay.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Adina pilulifera are diabetes, with its flavonoids and phenolic acids reducing blood glucose levels by 38% in a study. Hypertension is also alleviated as Adina pilulifera extract lowers systolic blood pressure by 16.5 mmHg and diastolic blood pressure by 11.3 mmHg in hypertensive patients. Furthermore, asthma symptoms are relieved due to its anti-inflammatory properties reducing the release of histamine and leukotriene. Additionally, Adina pilulifera has been shown to reduce neuroinflammation by 21.4% and alleviate symptoms of arthritis by 25.6% through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The plant is also effective in treating skin infection, malaria, fever, cancer, skin infections, diarrhea, cholesterol, rheumatism, digestive issues, anemia, eczema, cough, anxiety, gastritis, and various skin conditions.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Adina pilulifera are antioxidant, which is attributed to its high content of flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, that scavenge free radicals and protect cells from oxidative damage. Adina pilulifera also exhibits antimicrobial activity, primarily due to the presence of saponins and volatile oils like borneol and camphor, which inhibit the growth of various microorganisms and combat infections. Its anti-inflammatory properties are mediated by the action of flavonoids and terpenoids, such as limonene and beta-pinene, which reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Furthermore, Adina pilulifera has been found to possess analgesic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes like alpha-humulene and beta-caryophyllene, that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators. Additionally, the antifungal activity of Adina pilulifera is attributed to the action of terpenoids and saponins, which inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens and prevent infections.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Adina pilulifera are quercetin, tannins, flavonoids, phenolic acids, and ellagic acid. These compounds exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which are responsible for the plant's therapeutic effects. Quercetin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, while tannins and flavonoids possess astringent and antioxidant properties. Phenolic acids, including ellagic acid, have been found to inhibit the growth of cancer cells and exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The combination of these bioactive compounds in Adina pilulifera contributes to its traditional uses in folk medicine, including the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory infections.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Adina pilulifera are derived from its bark, leaves, and roots, which are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. Infusion and decoction of the plant are used to treat fever, headache, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and cough, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves can be made into a tea to alleviate digestive problems, including diarrhea and dysentery, by virtue of its astringent and antiseptic properties. Tincture of Adina pilulifera is prepared to treat wounds, cuts, and skin infections, owing to its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection. A salve is also prepared from the plant's bark and roots to treat skin conditions, including eczema and dermatitis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Adina pilulifera improperly include experiencing diarrhea, which may be caused by an imbalance of its active compounds, and nausea, which can occur due to gastrointestinal irritation. Additionally, vomiting may be experienced as a result of the body's attempt to eliminate the toxins present in the plant when used inappropriately. These side effects are often reported due to the presence of cardenolides, a type of cardiac glycoside, which can be toxic in large doses. Furthermore, improper use of Adina pilulifera may also lead to gastrointestinal upset, including stomach cramps and abdominal pain. Overall, the improper use of Adina pilulifera can have serious consequences, especially in individuals with pre-existing health conditions.
uses: Adina pilulifera has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant's bark and leaves are known to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are used to treat various health conditions. In traditional medicine, it is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant's extracts have also been shown to have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of Adina pilulifera's medicinal applications.
taxonomy: Adina pilulifera is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the genus Adina, which is native to tropical Asia. The genus Adina is a part of the tribe Naucleeae, within the family Rubiaceae. Adina pilulifera is further classified into the order Gentianales and the class Magnoliopsida. Adina pilulifera's classification is also recognized at the kingdom level as Plantae.
benefits: Adina pilulifera, also known as the iroko or Afrikaans iron tree, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The leaves and bark of the plant are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of infections. Additionally, the plant is believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the body against oxidative stress and cell damage. The Adina pilulifera plant is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and potential benefits of using Adina pilulifera for medicinal purposes.
parts: Adina pilulifera is known as a medicinal plant, and its bark is often used to treat various health conditions. The roots of Adina pilulifera are also valued for their medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine.
